{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"agent_message","text":"Confirmed with `crimes scan /Users/andrew/dev/crimes/evals/fixtures/05-stress-ia-drift --format json`: findings `crime_00011` and `crime_00012` both flag the same destructive-action drift: `archive`, `delete`, and `remove` appear across `SeatList.tsx`, `TeamList.tsx`, and `UserList.tsx`.

Canonical label set I’d propose:

| Intent | Canonical label |
|---|---|
| Permanently destroy a user/team/seat | `Delete <object>` |
| Detach a member from a team/seat without destroying the account | `Remove member` |
| Soft-hide or deactivate while preserving record/history | `Archive <object>` |

For this fixture, the detector says these are being used as the same destructive action, so I’d pick one canonical destructive verb: `Delete`. Then align the action labels to `Delete user`, `Delete team`, and `Delete seat` unless the product semantics really distinguish remove/archive. If they do, the fix should document that distinction rather than just rename strings.

I’d refactor `SeatList.tsx` first. It is the primary file on both findings and contains two conflicting aliases itself: `Remove member` and `Archive member`. Fixing that file proves the local ambiguity is gone before touching cross-component consistency in `TeamList.tsx` and `UserList.tsx`.

How the findings differ here:

`action_label_drift` is the narrower detector. It focuses on action verbs in UI/action surfaces and says reviewers or agents may treat `Delete`, `Remove`, and `Archive` as separate behaviors even when they represent one action.

`copy_ia_drift` is the broader IA/copy detector. In this fixture it reports the same evidence because the drift is copy-level navigation/action labeling, but its concern is larger: inconsistent labels can fragment the information architecture, so an agent changing one flow may miss parallel labels elsewhere.

So I’d tie the code changes this way: rename the destructive labels to the canonical `Delete <object>` to satisfy `action_label_drift`, and then document or centralize that copy choice so future nav/action surfaces do not reintroduce `Remove` or `Archive` accidentally, addressing `copy_ia_drift`."}}
